title: UI and UX Design Foundations Quiz

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "User interface" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The visible and interactive parts of a product
wrongAnswers: A research-based profile representing a user type
Organizing content so people can find and understand it
A visual difference that helps elements stand apart

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "User experience" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The overall experience a person has using a product
wrongAnswers: The steps a user takes to reach a goal
Controls that help users move through a product
Arranging elements to show importance

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "Persona" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A research-based profile representing a user type
wrongAnswers: A low-detail layout plan for an interface
A cue that suggests how something can be used
Using familiar patterns across an interface

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "User journey" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: The steps a user takes to reach a goal
wrongAnswers: An interactive or visual model used to test a design
A system response that confirms an action or state
A design state that communicates a problem

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "Wireframe" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A low-detail layout plan for an interface
wrongAnswers: Observing users to find design problems
Designing products for people with varied abilities
A design state shown when there is no content yet

listOfQuestions.question: In UI and UX Design, what does "Prototype" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: An interactive or visual model used to test a design
wrongAnswers: Organizing content so people can find and understand it
A visual difference that helps elements stand apart
Helping new users begin using a product

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: Observing users to find design problems
wrongAnswers: Controls that help users move through a product
Arranging elements to show importance
Short interface text that guides users

wrongAnswers: A cue that suggests how something can be used

A shared set of reusable design rules and components
